name: Calculus
description: An introduction to single-variable calculus covering limits, derivatives, and integrals. Students develop conceptual understanding and computational fluency in differentiation rules, applications of derivatives (optimization, related rates, curve sketching), the Fundamental Theorem of Calculus, integration techniques, and an introduction to differential equations and sequences — the foundation for all advanced STEM study.

modules.title: Limits
modules.objectives: Evaluate limits graphically, numerically, and algebraically; apply limit laws; distinguish between a function value and a limit; compute one-sided limits.
modules.assignment: Evaluate 10 limits algebraically using limit laws. Sketch a graph showing left-hand and right-hand limits that differ. Write a paragraph explaining what a limit means intuitively.

modules.title: Continuity
modules.objectives: Define continuity at a point using the three-condition definition; identify types of discontinuities; apply the Intermediate Value Theorem.
modules.videoNote: Khan Academy — Continuity at a Point
modules.video2Note: Professor Leonard — Continuity and the Intermediate Value Theorem
modules.practiceNote: Khan Academy — Continuity at a Point (15 problems)
modules.assignment: Classify 6 discontinuities as removable, jump, or infinite. Use the IVT to prove a root exists in a given interval for 3 functions.

modules.title: The Derivative: Definition
modules.objectives: Define the derivative as a limit of a difference quotient; interpret the derivative as slope of tangent line and instantaneous rate of change; use the definition to compute derivatives.
modules.readingNote: OpenStax Calculus Volume 1 — Ch 3.1: Defining the Derivative
modules.videoNote: Khan Academy — The Derivative: A Concept
modules.video2Note: Professor Leonard — Definition of the Derivative
modules.practiceNote: Khan Academy — Derivative as a Limit (15 problems)
modules.assignment: Use the limit definition to find the derivative of 5 functions. Write the equation of the tangent line to 3 curves at given points.

modules.title: Differentiation Rules
modules.objectives: Apply the power rule, constant rule, sum/difference rule, and product and quotient rules; differentiate polynomial, rational, and radical functions.
modules.readingNote: OpenStax Calculus Volume 1 — Ch 3.3: Differentiation Rules
modules.practiceNote: Khan Academy — Power Rule Practice (25 problems)
modules.assignment: Differentiate 15 functions using power, product, and quotient rules. Find the second derivative of 5 functions.

modules.title: The Chain Rule
modules.objectives: Apply the chain rule to differentiate composite functions; combine the chain rule with product and quotient rules; differentiate trigonometric, exponential, and logarithmic functions.
modules.readingNote: OpenStax Calculus Volume 1 — Ch 3.6: The Chain Rule
modules.practiceNote: Khan Academy — Chain Rule Practice (20 problems)
modules.assignment: Differentiate 12 composite functions using the chain rule. Apply the chain rule combined with product/quotient rules for 5 complex functions.

modules.title: Applications of Derivatives
modules.objectives: Use the first and second derivative tests to find extrema; determine intervals of increase/decrease and concavity; sketch curves using derivative information.
modules.readingNote: OpenStax Calculus Volume 1 — Ch 4.3–4.5: Curve Sketching
modules.videoNote: Khan Academy — Relative Maxima and Minima
modules.video2Note: Professor Leonard — Increasing/Decreasing and First Derivative Test
modules.practiceNote: Khan Academy — Relative Maxima and Minima (20 problems)
modules.assignment: For 5 functions: find critical points, classify them, determine intervals of increase/decrease and concavity, and sketch the graph.

modules.title: Optimization
modules.objectives: Set up and solve optimization problems using derivatives; identify absolute extrema on closed intervals; apply the closed interval method.
modules.readingNote: OpenStax Calculus Volume 1 — Ch 4.7: Applied Optimization Problems
modules.assignment: Solve 6 classic optimization problems (fencing, box, can). Write and solve your own optimization problem from a real-world scenario.

modules.title: Related Rates
modules.objectives: Set up equations relating quantities that change with time; use implicit differentiation to find related rates; solve applied problems involving geometric quantities.
modules.readingNote: OpenStax Calculus Volume 1 — Ch 4.1: Related Rates
modules.videoNote: Khan Academy — Rates of Change Between Related Quantities
modules.assignment: Solve 6 related rates problems (ladder, balloon, shadow, water tank). Diagram each situation and clearly state all given rates.

modules.title: Introduction to Integrals
modules.objectives: Understand the antiderivative and indefinite integral; use the notation integral f(x)dx; apply basic antiderivative rules; use sigma notation and Riemann sums to approximate area.
modules.readingNote: OpenStax Calculus Volume 1 — Ch 5.1–5.3: Approximating Areas and Definite Integrals
modules.videoNote: Khan Academy — Introduction to Integral Calculus
modules.video2Note: Professor Leonard — Introduction to Integration (Riemann Sums)
modules.practiceNote: Khan Academy — Riemann Sums Practice (15 problems)
modules.assignment: Compute left, right, and midpoint Riemann sums with n=4 for 3 functions. Find antiderivatives of 10 basic functions.

modules.title: The Fundamental Theorem of Calculus
modules.objectives: State and apply both parts of the Fundamental Theorem of Calculus; evaluate definite integrals using antiderivatives; differentiate integrals with variable limits.
modules.readingNote: OpenStax Calculus Volume 1 — Ch 5.3: The Fundamental Theorem of Calculus
modules.videoNote: Khan Academy — Fundamental Theorem of Calculus
modules.video2Note: Professor Leonard — The Fundamental Theorem of Calculus
modules.assignment: Evaluate 12 definite integrals using the FTC. Apply FTC Part 1 to differentiate 5 integral functions with variable upper limits.

modules.title: Integration Techniques
modules.objectives: Apply u-substitution to evaluate indefinite and definite integrals; recognize when to use substitution; evaluate integrals of trigonometric, exponential, and logarithmic functions.
modules.assignment: Evaluate 15 integrals using u-substitution (mix of indefinite and definite). Identify the appropriate u for 5 tricky integrals.

modules.title: Applications of Integration
modules.objectives: Compute areas between curves; find average value of a function; solve basic problems involving distance, displacement, and net change using integrals.
modules.readingNote: OpenStax Calculus Volume 1 — Ch 6.1: Areas Between Curves
modules.practiceNote: Khan Academy — Area Between Curves (15 problems)
modules.assignment: Find the area between 5 pairs of curves. Compute the average value of 3 functions on a given interval. Solve 2 net change (displacement) problems.

modules.title: Introduction to Differential Equations
modules.objectives: Identify and classify ordinary differential equations; verify solutions; solve separable ODEs by separating variables and integrating; model exponential growth and decay.
modules.readingNote: OpenStax Calculus Volume 2 — Ch 4.1–4.3: Differential Equations Basics
modules.video2Note: Professor Leonard — Differential Equations: Separation of Variables
modules.practiceNote: Khan Academy — Separable Differential Equations (15 problems)
modules.assignment: Solve 8 separable differential equations with initial conditions. Set up and solve exponential growth and decay models for 3 real scenarios.

modules.title: Sequences & Series Introduction
modules.objectives: Define sequences and series; determine convergence or divergence of geometric and arithmetic sequences; compute partial sums; apply the geometric series formula.
modules.readingNote: OpenStax Calculus Volume 2 — Ch 5.1–5.3: Sequences and Series
modules.video2Note: Professor Leonard — Introduction to Sequences and Series
modules.practiceNote: Khan Academy — Convergent and Divergent Sequences (20 problems)
modules.assignment: Determine convergence for 8 sequences. Compute the sum of 3 convergent geometric series. Write a 1-page explanation of the difference between a sequence and a series.

quizQuestions.question: What is the limit of (x^2 - 4)/(x - 2) as x approaches 2?
If lim f(x) = 3 and lim g(x) = 2 as x->a, what is lim [f(x) + g(x)]?
quizQuestions.explanation: By the sum limit law: lim [f(x) + g(x)] = lim f(x) + lim g(x) = 3 + 2 = 5.

quizQuestions.question: What is lim (sin x)/x as x approaches 0?
quizQuestions.explanation: lim (sin x)/x = 1 as x->0 is a fundamental limit in calculus.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The limit of (x^2 - 4)/(x - 2) as x approaches 2 is "4".
quizQuestions.explanation: True. 4 is correct.

quizQuestions.question: What are the three conditions for f to be continuous at x = a?
options: f(a) defined, lim exists, lim = f(a)
f(a) exists, f is differentiable, limit is finite
f is smooth, has no corners, lim = 0
f(a) = 0, f is increasing, lim = f(a)
quizQuestions.answer: f(a) defined, lim exists, lim = f(a)
quizQuestions.explanation: Continuity at a requires: (1) f(a) is defined, (2) lim f(x) as x->a exists, (3) the limit equals f(a).

quizQuestions.question: Which type of discontinuity can be 'fixed' by redefining f at one point?
quizQuestions.explanation: A removable discontinuity occurs when the limit exists but does not equal the function value. It can be removed by redefining f at that point.

quizQuestions.question: The Intermediate Value Theorem guarantees a root of f(x) = x^3 - 2 in [1, 2] because:
options: f(1) < 0 and f(2) > 0 and f is continuous
quizQuestions.answer: f(1) < 0 and f(2) > 0 and f is continuous
quizQuestions.explanation: f(1) = -1 < 0 and f(2) = 6 > 0. Since f is continuous, IVT guarantees a c in (1,2) with f(c) = 0.

quizQuestions.question: The function f(x) = 1/x has what type of discontinuity at x = 0?
quizQuestions.explanation: f(x) = 1/x approaches +/-infinity as x->0, creating a vertical asymptote and an infinite discontinuity.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The three conditions for f to be continuous at x = a is "f(a) defined, lim exists, lim = f(a)".
quizQuestions.explanation: True. f(a) defined, lim exists, lim = f(a) is correct.

quizQuestions.question: The derivative f'(a) is defined as which limit?
quizQuestions.explanation: The derivative is defined as f'(a) = lim_{h->0} [f(a+h) - f(a)] / h.

quizQuestions.question: Geometrically, the derivative f'(a) represents:
options: The area under f at x = a
The slope of the secant line
The slope of the tangent line to f at x = a
quizQuestions.answer: The slope of the tangent line to f at x = a
quizQuestions.explanation: f'(a) is the slope of the tangent line to the graph of f at the point (a, f(a)).

quizQuestions.question: Use the definition to find the derivative of f(x) = x^2.
quizQuestions.explanation: lim [(x+h)^2 - x^2]/h = lim [2xh + h^2]/h = lim [2x + h] = 2x as h->0.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "The derivative f'(a) is defined as which limit" is: lim [f(a+h) - f(a)] / h as h->0.
quizQuestions.explanation: True. lim [f(a+h) - f(a)] / h as h->0 is correct.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "Geometrically, the derivative f'(a) represents:" is: The area under f at x = a.
quizQuestions.explanation: False. The correct answer is: The slope of the tangent line to f at x = a.

quizQuestions.question: What is the derivative of f(x) = x^5?
quizQuestions.explanation: By the power rule, d/dx[x^n] = n*x^(n-1). So d/dx[x^5] = 5x^4.

quizQuestions.question: What is the derivative of f(x) = 3x^4 - 2x^2 + 7?
What is the product rule for d/dx[f(x)*g(x)]?
quizQuestions.explanation: Product rule: d/dx[f*g] = f'*g + f*g'. (First times derivative of second plus second times derivative of first.)

quizQuestions.question: What is the derivative of f(x) = sin(x)?
quizQuestions.explanation: d/dx[sin(x)] = cos(x) is a fundamental derivative formula.

quizQuestions.question: Fill in the blank: ___ is the derivative of f(x) = sin(x).
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: cos(x).

quizQuestions.question: State the chain rule for d/dx[f(g(x))].
quizQuestions.explanation: Chain rule: d/dx[f(g(x))] = f'(g(x)) * g'(x). Derivative of outer (evaluated at inner) times derivative of inner.

quizQuestions.question: Find d/dx[sin(3x)].
quizQuestions.explanation: Chain rule: outer is sin(u), inner is u = 3x. d/dx[sin(3x)] = cos(3x) * 3 = 3cos(3x).

quizQuestions.question: Find d/dx[(x^2 + 1)^4].
What is d/dx[e^(x^2)]?
quizQuestions.explanation: Chain rule: d/dx[e^u] = e^u * u', where u = x^2. So d/dx[e^(x^2)] = e^(x^2) * 2x = 2x*e^(x^2).

quizQuestions.question: Find d/dx[ln(x^2 + 1)].
quizQuestions.explanation: d/dx[ln(u)] = u'/u. Here u = x^2+1, u' = 2x. So the derivative is 2x/(x^2+1).

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "State the chain rule for d/dx[f(g(x))]." is: f'(g(x))*g'(x).
quizQuestions.explanation: True. f'(g(x))*g'(x) is correct.

quizQuestions.question: A critical point of f occurs where:
quizQuestions.explanation: Critical points occur where the derivative is zero or undefined — these are candidates for relative extrema.

quizQuestions.question: If f'(x) changes from positive to negative at x = c, then f has a:
quizQuestions.explanation: First Derivative Test: if f' goes from + to - at c, then f has a local maximum at c.

quizQuestions.question: If f''(x) > 0 on an interval, then f is:
quizQuestions.explanation: f''(x) > 0 means the first derivative is increasing, which means f is concave up (bends upward like a cup).

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "A critical point of f occurs where:" is: f'(x) = 0 or f'(x) is undefined.
quizQuestions.explanation: True. f'(x) = 0 or f'(x) is undefined is correct.

quizQuestions.question: Fill in the blank: Complete: In the context of the topic, ___ refers to "an inflection point is where:."
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: f changes concavity.

quizQuestions.question: You have 100 meters of fencing for a rectangular garden against a wall (one side needs no fencing). What dimensions maximize the area?
quizQuestions.explanation: With constraint x + 2y = 100 (x is the side along wall), Area = xy = (100-2y)y. dA/dy = 100-4y = 0, y = 25. x = 50. Max area = 50*25 = 1250 m^2.

quizQuestions.question: Absolute extrema on a closed interval [a, b] can occur at:
quizQuestions.explanation: By the Closed Interval Method, absolute extrema occur at critical points inside [a,b] or at the endpoints a and b.

quizQuestions.question: To minimize the surface area of a can with fixed volume, you set up the objective function and then:
options: Find its derivative and set equal to zero
quizQuestions.answer: Find its derivative and set equal to zero
quizQuestions.explanation: To minimize or maximize, differentiate the objective function and solve f'(x) = 0 to find critical points.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "You have 100 meters of fencing for a rectangular garden against a wall (one side needs no fencing). What dimensions maximize the area" is: 25 m x 50 m.
quizQuestions.explanation: True. 25 m x 50 m is correct.

quizQuestions.question: Fill in the blank: Complete: In the context of the topic, ___ refers to "which theorem guarantees an absolute maximum exists for a continuous f on a closed interval [a,b]."
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Extreme Value Theorem.

quizQuestions.question: A spherical balloon is being inflated. If the radius increases at 2 cm/s, how fast is the volume increasing when r = 3 cm?
In a related rates problem, implicit differentiation is applied with respect to:
quizQuestions.explanation: In related rates, we differentiate both sides of an equation with respect to time t to find how rates are related.

quizQuestions.question: A 10-ft ladder slides down a wall. When the top is 6 ft high and sliding at 2 ft/s, how fast is the base moving away?
Which equation relates the sides of a right triangle in a typical related rates ladder problem?
quizQuestions.explanation: The Pythagorean theorem x^2 + y^2 = L^2 relates the horizontal and vertical legs to the fixed ladder length.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "A spherical balloon is being inflated. If the radius increases at 2 cm/s, how fast is the volume increasing when r = 3 cm" is: 72pi cm^3/s.
quizQuestions.explanation: True. 72pi cm^3/s is correct.

quizQuestions.question: What is the antiderivative of f(x) = 3x^2?
quizQuestions.explanation: The antiderivative reverses differentiation: d/dx[x^3] = 3x^2, so the antiderivative of 3x^2 is x^3 + C.

quizQuestions.question: A right Riemann sum with n rectangles over [a, b] uses which x-values?
quizQuestions.explanation: A right Riemann sum evaluates the function at the right endpoint of each subinterval.

quizQuestions.question: As n (number of rectangles) approaches infinity, the Riemann sum approaches:
options: The exact area under the curve
quizQuestions.answer: The exact area under the curve
quizQuestions.explanation: The definite integral is defined as the limit of Riemann sums as the number of subintervals approaches infinity.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The antiderivative of f(x) = 3x^2 is "x^3 + C".
quizQuestions.explanation: True. x^3 + C is correct.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "A right Riemann sum with n rectangles over [a, b] uses which x-values" is: Left endpoints of each subinterval.
quizQuestions.explanation: False. The correct answer is: Right endpoints of each subinterval.

quizQuestions.question: FTC Part 2: How do you evaluate the definite integral from a to b of f(x) dx?
options: Find F(a) - F(b) where F' = f
Find F(b) - F(a) where F' = f
Compute lim of Riemann sums only
quizQuestions.answer: Find F(b) - F(a) where F' = f
quizQuestions.explanation: FTC Part 2: integral from a to b of f(x) dx = F(b) - F(a), where F is any antiderivative of f.

quizQuestions.question: Evaluate the integral from 0 to 2 of 3x^2 dx.
quizQuestions.explanation: Antiderivative is x^3. F(2) - F(0) = 8 - 0 = 8.

quizQuestions.question: FTC Part 1 states: d/dx [integral from a to x of f(t) dt] = ?
quizQuestions.explanation: FTC Part 1: The derivative of an integral with a variable upper limit equals the integrand evaluated at x.

quizQuestions.question: What is integral from 0 to pi of sin(x) dx?
quizQuestions.explanation: Antiderivative of sin(x) is -cos(x). [-cos(pi)] - [-cos(0)] = -(-1) - (-1) = 1 + 1 = 2.

quizQuestions.question: Fill in the blank: Complete: In the context of the topic, ___ refers to "using ftc part 1, find d/dx [integral from 1 to x of sqrt(t) dt].."
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: sqrt(x).

quizQuestions.question: Fill in the blank: ___ is integral from 0 to pi of sin(x) dx.
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: 2.

quizQuestions.question: To integrate integral of 2x*(x^2+1)^4 dx, what is the best substitution?
quizQuestions.explanation: Let u = x^2+1, then du = 2x dx. The integral becomes integral of u^4 du = u^5/5 + C = (x^2+1)^5/5 + C.

quizQuestions.question: Evaluate integral of cos(5x) dx.
quizQuestions.explanation: u = 5x, du = 5 dx. integral of cos(5x) dx = (1/5)integral of cos(u) du = (1/5)sin(u) + C = sin(5x)/5 + C.

quizQuestions.question: What is integral of e^(3x) dx?
quizQuestions.explanation: u = 3x, du = 3 dx. integral of e^(3x) dx = (1/3)integral of e^u du = (1/3)e^(3x) + C.

quizQuestions.question: What is integral of 1/x dx?
quizQuestions.explanation: integral of 1/x dx = ln|x| + C. This is the special case excluded from the power rule (n = -1).

quizQuestions.question: When using u-substitution on a definite integral, you must:
options: Change the limits to u-values OR substitute back before using original limits
Multiply by du/dx at the end
quizQuestions.answer: Change the limits to u-values OR substitute back before using original limits
quizQuestions.explanation: When doing u-substitution on definite integrals, either convert the limits to u-values (preferred) or back-substitute to x before evaluating.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "To integrate integral of 2x*(x^2+1)^4 dx, what is the best substitution" is: u = x^2 + 1.
quizQuestions.explanation: True. u = x^2 + 1 is correct.

quizQuestions.question: The area between f(x) and g(x) from a to b where f >= g is:
options: integral from a to b of f(x) dx
integral from a to b of g(x) dx
integral from a to b of [f(x) - g(x)] dx
integral from a to b of [f(x) + g(x)] dx
quizQuestions.answer: integral from a to b of [f(x) - g(x)] dx
quizQuestions.explanation: Area between curves = integral of (top curve - bottom curve) dx over the interval.

quizQuestions.question: Find the area between y = x and y = x^2 from 0 to 1.
quizQuestions.explanation: integral from 0 to 1 of (x - x^2) dx = [x^2/2 - x^3/3] from 0 to 1 = 1/2 - 1/3 = 1/6.

quizQuestions.question: The average value of f on [a, b] is:
options: 1/(b-a) * integral from a to b of f(x) dx
integral from a to b of f(x) dx
quizQuestions.answer: 1/(b-a) * integral from a to b of f(x) dx
quizQuestions.explanation: The average value of f on [a,b] = (1/(b-a)) * integral from a to b of f(x) dx.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "The area between f(x) and g(x) from a to b where f >= g is:" is: integral from a to b of [f(x) - g(x)] dx.
quizQuestions.explanation: True. integral from a to b of [f(x) - g(x)] dx is correct.

quizQuestions.question: Fill in the blank: Complete: In the context of the topic, ___ refers to "net change theorem states that integral from a to b of f'(x) dx equals:."
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: F(b) - F(a).

quizQuestions.question: Fill in the blank: Complete: In the context of the topic, ___ refers to "if v(t) = 3t^2 is the velocity at time t, the displacement from t=0 to t=2 is:."
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: 8.

quizQuestions.question: A separable differential equation can be written as:
quizQuestions.explanation: A separable ODE can be written as dy/dx = f(x)*g(y), allowing separation: g(y)^(-1) dy = f(x) dx.

quizQuestions.question: Solve dy/dx = 2x with y(0) = 3.
quizQuestions.explanation: dy = 2x dx -> y = x^2 + C. Using y(0) = 3: 3 = 0 + C, so C = 3. Thus y = x^2 + 3.

quizQuestions.question: The exponential growth model is dy/dt = ky. Its solution is:
quizQuestions.explanation: Separating: dy/y = k dt, integrating gives ln(y) = kt + C, so y = y0 * e^(kt) where y0 is the initial value.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "A separable differential equation can be written as:" is: dy/dx = f(x) * g(y).
quizQuestions.explanation: True. dy/dx = f(x) * g(y) is correct.

quizQuestions.question: A sequence {a_n} converges if:
options: lim a_n = L for some finite L as n -> infinity
quizQuestions.answer: lim a_n = L for some finite L as n -> infinity
quizQuestions.explanation: A sequence converges if its terms approach a finite limit as n approaches infinity.

quizQuestions.question: Does the sequence a_n = 1/n converge?
quizQuestions.explanation: lim (1/n) = 0 as n -> infinity. The sequence converges to 0.

quizQuestions.question: A geometric series sum from n=0 to infinity of a*r^n converges when:
quizQuestions.explanation: A geometric series converges if and only if the common ratio satisfies |r| < 1. When it converges, the sum is a/(1-r).

quizQuestions.question: What is the sum of the infinite geometric series 1 + 1/2 + 1/4 + 1/8 + ...?
True or False: The answer to "A sequence {a_n} converges if:" is: lim a_n = L for some finite L as n -> infinity.
quizQuestions.explanation: True. lim a_n = L for some finite L as n -> infinity is correct.

questions.question: Evaluate: lim (x^2 - 9)/(x - 3) as x -> 3.
Is f(x) = (x^2-4)/(x-2) continuous at x = 2?
options: Yes, all rational functions are continuous
No, because the limit does not exist
questions.explanation: f(2) is undefined (0/0 form), creating a removable discontinuity (hole) at x = 2.

questions.question: Using the limit definition, what is the derivative of f(x) = 2x + 1?
True or False: The answer to "Find the derivative of f(x) = 4x^3 - 6x^2 + 5x - 1." is: 12x^2 - 12x + 5.
Find d/dx [(x^2)(sin(x))].
Find d/dx [cos(x^3)].
questions.explanation: Chain rule: d/dx[cos(u)] = -sin(u)*u', where u = x^3, u' = 3x^2. So -sin(x^3)*3x^2.

questions.question: For f(x) = x^3 - 3x, on which interval is f decreasing?
True or False: The answer to "A box with a square base and no top has volume 32 cubic feet. What side length x minimizes total surface area" is: x = 2 ft.
questions.explanation: False. The correct answer is: x = 4 ft.

questions.question: Find all critical points of f(x) = x^3 - 12x + 1.
What is lim as x -> infinity of (3x^2 + 1)/(x^2 - 2)?
questions.explanation: Divide numerator and denominator by x^2: (3 + 1/x^2)/(1 - 2/x^2) -> 3/1 = 3 as x -> infinity.

questions.question: A particle's position is s(t) = t^3 - 3t^2. Its velocity at t = 2 is:
True or False: The answer to "If f''(x) < 0 on an interval, f is:" is: Concave down.
The IVT guarantees a solution to f(x) = 0 in (1, 2) for f(x) = x^2 - 2. Why?
options: f is a polynomial and f(1) = -1 < 0 < 2 = f(2)
f(1) < 0 and f(2) > 0 and f is continuous
The graph crosses zero by visual inspection
questions.answer: f(1) < 0 and f(2) > 0 and f is continuous
questions.explanation: f(1) = -1 < 0 and f(2) = 2 > 0. Since f is continuous, IVT guarantees c in (1,2) with f(c) = 0.

questions.question: Find the tangent line to f(x) = x^2 at x = 3.
For a related rates problem, a stone dropped in water creates ripples. If the radius grows at 3 m/s, how fast does the area grow when r = 5 m?
True or False: The answer to "Evaluate: integral from 1 to 3 of (2x + 1) dx." is: 8.
What is integral of (x^2 + 1) dx?
questions.explanation: integral(x^2) dx = x^3/3 and integral(1) dx = x. So the result is x^3/3 + x + C.

questions.question: Using u-substitution, evaluate integral of x*sqrt(x^2+1) dx.
questions.explanation: u = x^2+1, du = 2x dx. integral of x*sqrt(x^2+1) dx = (1/2)integral of sqrt(u) du = (1/2)*(2/3)*u^(3/2) + C = (1/3)(x^2+1)^(3/2) + C.

questions.question: d/dx [integral from 0 to x^2 of sin(t) dt] = ?
questions.explanation: By FTC Part 1 and chain rule: if g(x) = x^2, then d/dx[integral from 0 to g(x) of sin(t) dt] = sin(g(x))*g'(x) = sin(x^2)*2x.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "Find the area between y = x^2 and y = x from x = 0 to x = 1." is: 1/6.
Solve the separable ODE: dy/dx = y with y(0) = 2.
What is the average value of f(x) = x^2 on [0, 3]?
questions.explanation: Average = (1/3)*integral from 0 to 3 of x^2 dx = (1/3)*[x^3/3] from 0 to 3 = (1/3)*9 = 3.

questions.question: Does the geometric series 3 + 3/2 + 3/4 + 3/8 + ... converge? If so, what is its sum?
True or False: The answer to "A radioactive substance decays by 10% per year. If P0 = 500 grams, what is the amount after 10 years" is: 500*e^(-10).
Evaluate integral of e^x*sin(e^x) dx.
questions.explanation: u = e^x, du = e^x dx. integral of sin(u) du = -cos(u) + C = -cos(e^x) + C.

questions.question: Find the absolute maximum of f(x) = x^3 - 3x on [-2, 2].
questions.explanation: f'(x) = 3x^2 - 3 = 0 -> x = +/-1. f(-2)=-2, f(-1)=2, f(1)=-2, f(2)=2. Absolute max = 2, at both x=-1 and x=2. Among listed, f=2 at x=-1 is correct.

questions.question: If a_n = (2n+1)/n, what does this sequence converge to?
questions.explanation: a_n = 2 + 1/n. As n -> infinity, 1/n -> 0. So a_n -> 2.

questions.question: Find d/dx [tan(x^2 + 1)].
Two cars are at an intersection. Car A moves north at 50 mph and Car B moves east at 120 mph. How fast does the distance between them increase (initially)?
Using the MVT, if f(0) = 1 and f(4) = 9 and f is continuous on [0,4] and differentiable on (0,4), there exists c in (0,4) with f'(c) = ?
What is the displacement of a particle with velocity v(t) = t^2 - t from t=0 to t=3?
questions.explanation: Displacement = integral from 0 to 3 of (t^2 - t) dt = [t^3/3 - t^2/2] from 0 to 3 = (27/3 - 9/2) = 9 - 4.5 = 4.5 = 9/2.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "Evaluate: integral from -1 to 1 of x^3 dx." is: 1/2.
A company's profit function is P(x) = -x^2 + 40x - 300, where x is units sold. How many units maximize profit?
questions.explanation: P'(x) = -2x + 40 = 0 -> x = 20. P''(x) = -2 < 0 confirms a maximum.

questions.question: What does the notation integral from a to b of f(x) dx represent geometrically when f(x) >= 0?
options: The area under the curve f from x=a to x=b
The average rate of change of f
The maximum value of f on [a,b]
questions.answer: The area under the curve f from x=a to x=b
questions.explanation: When f(x) >= 0, the definite integral represents the area of the region between the graph of f and the x-axis from a to b.

questions.question: Solve: dy/dx = 3x^2*y with y(0) = 1.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: y = e^(x^3).
